content.text:
## What the current changes *mean* (not just file names)

You’ve got a **UI refactor of the dashboard** in progress.

- `next-app/components/remote-shell.tsx`  
  Big rewrite from a simple 3-card page into a **sidebar + tabbed layout** (`messages`/`members`), adds user profile section with a dialog, and keeps machine/command logic but rearranges where it renders. Also now expects `userEmail` prop.

- `next-app/app/dashboard/page.tsx`  
  Small wiring change: now passes both `userName` and `userEmail` into `RemoteShell`.

- `next-app/app/layout.tsx`  
  App shell now forces dark mode (`<html className="... dark">`) and wraps app with `TooltipProvider` so new tooltip-based UI components work globally.

- `next-app/app/globals.css`  
  Theme tokens were heavily replaced (new light/dark color palette, sidebar-specific variables, radius change), plus `letter-spacing` token applied to `body`. So visual design/theme system changed significantly.

## New untracked files

These are mostly **new shadcn/Radix-style UI primitives** added to support that refactor:

- `next-app/components/ui/{dialog,sheet,sidebar,skeleton,tabs,tooltip}.tsx`
- `next-app/hooks/use-mobile.ts` (used by sidebar for responsive behavior)

And these are tooling/session artifacts, not app features:

- `.entire/` (logs/metadata/tmp/settings)
- `.factory/` (local agent settings)

If you want, I can also give you a **“ready-to-commit vs should-ignore”** split for these untracked files.
stage and commit everything and push. We will soon start working on agents
